content:
Did Claude start showing sponsored content? What we found in a Singapore logistics audit — and why it matters for B2B visibility

2 min read
Just now

--

During a Singapore logistics audit, Claude surfaced a citation hover label reading “Sponsored content.” We checked whether anyone else had reported it.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

We are running a four-week experiment tracking how B2B SaaS providers appear across ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, and Google AI Overview. The prompts are written the way buyers actually research — not brand queries, not SEO keywords.
One logistics prompt from a Singapore IP returned an answer that sounded authoritative. Fragmentation, SME digitisation gaps, labour constraints. Standard Claude.
Then we hovered a source chip.
Logistics Challenges for SMEs: Finding a Solution | Value Chain Asia
Secondary label: Sponsored content
The linked page is a native advertorial for OnRobot’s D:PLOY cobot platform. Claude used it to ground claims about automation adoption in Singapore — while other citations on the same answer (FreightWaves, Haulio, Aspire) carried no sponsored label.
Anthropic published a formal ad-free pledge in February 2026, timed against ChatGPT’s sponsored placement launch. Industry research puts Claude’s web search at roughly 87% alignment with Brave’s organic results. We found no other public report of Claude citation hovers explicitly labeled “Sponsored content.”
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Claude result with a Sponsored Content CitationSo what is happening? Probably not Claude selling ads. More likely metadata passthrough: the publisher marks the article as sponsored; Claude displays it in the hover card. The practical problem is the same either way — buyers may treat AI answers as neutral research while part of the evidence is paid trade media, and most marketing teams never check.
This piece documents what we saw, what we could and could not find online, and a simple protocol for auditing citation labels in your own category.
Read the full audit with screenshot and FAQ:
https://www.citablehq.com/visibility/claude-sponsored-content-citation-label-audit-2026/
